name: salesforce-development
description: Expert patterns for Salesforce platform development including Lightning Web Components (LWC), Apex triggers and classes, REST/Bulk APIs, Connected Apps, and Salesforce DX with scratch orgs and 2nd generation packages (2GP). Use when: salesforce, sfdc, apex, lwc, lightning web components.
capabilities: Generate Lightning Web Component patterns with @wire service integration
Create bulkified Apex triggers using handler pattern architecture
Design Queueable Apex jobs for async processing with job chaining
Identify and correct SOQL and DML anti-patterns in loops
Provide guidance on Salesforce DX scratch org workflows
Explain REST and Bulk API integration patterns for Connected Apps
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at lines 3 and 55. Manual review confirms these are FALSE POSITIVES - the flagged lines contain only documentation text (frontmatter description and generic usage statement). This is a knowledge-based skill providing Salesforce development guidance with no executable code, network access, file operations, or external command execution. No security concerns identified.
valueStatement: Salesforce developers need reliable patterns for building scalable CRM solutions. This skill provides proven architectures for Lightning Web Components, bulkified Apex triggers, and async processing with Queueable Apex.
userTitle: Build Salesforce Apps with LWC and Apex
limitations: Does not execute Apex code or deploy to Salesforce orgs directly
Cannot access live Salesforce metadata or org configuration
Patterns require manual implementation and testing in target org
Does not replace Salesforce documentation or Trailhead learning resources

useCases.targetUser: Salesforce developers building custom UI components
useCases.title: LWC Development for Salesforce Developers
useCases.description: Generate production-ready Lightning Web Components with reactive data binding using @wire service and Lightning Data Service patterns.

useCases.targetUser: Salesforce admins transitioning to programmatic development
useCases.title: Apex Trigger Architecture for Admins
useCases.description: Create bulkified trigger handlers that process 200+ records per transaction while preventing recursion and maintaining separation of concerns.

useCases.targetUser: Technical architects designing scalable Salesforce solutions
useCases.title: Async Processing Design for Architects
useCases.description: Implement Queueable Apex patterns for complex async workflows with monitoring via AsyncApexJob and controlled job chaining.

promptTemplates.title: Beginner - LWC Component Pattern
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a Lightning Web Component that displays Account records using the @wire decorator with Lightning Data Service. Include error handling and loading states.
promptTemplates.scenario: Learning LWC reactive data binding patterns

promptTemplates.title: Intermediate - Bulkified Trigger Handler
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an Apex trigger handler for Opportunity that handles before insert and before update events. Ensure bulkification for 200+ records and include recursion prevention logic.

promptTemplates.title: Advanced - Queueable Job Chain
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a Queueable Apex chain that processes large data volumes in batches. Include error handling, AsyncApexJob monitoring, and proper governor limit management across chained jobs.

promptTemplates.title: Expert - API Integration Pattern
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a Connected App integration pattern that uses REST API with OAuth 2.0. Include callout patterns, authentication handling, and bulk data synchronization with external systems.
promptTemplates.scenario: Integrating Salesforce with external enterprise systems

bestPractices: Always bulkify Apex code to handle 200+ records per transaction and avoid governor limit exceptions
Use @wire decorator for reactive LWC data binding to leverage Salesforce performance optimizations
Implement handler pattern for triggers to separate concerns, enable testing, and prevent recursion
antiPatterns: Executing SOQL queries inside for loops - causes governor limit violations at scale
Performing DML operations inside loops - inefficient and risks limit exceptions
Hardcoding record IDs in Apex code - breaks across sandboxes and production environments

faq.question: What is the @wire decorator in LWC?
faq.answer: The @wire decorator enables reactive data binding in Lightning Web Components. It automatically refreshes component data when underlying records change and integrates with Lightning Data Service for caching and offline support.

faq.question: Why must Apex triggers be bulkified?
faq.answer: Salesforce processes records in batches of up to 200 per transaction. Non-bulkified triggers with queries or DML inside loops will hit governor limits when processing large data volumes, causing runtime exceptions.

faq.question: What is the difference between Batch Apex and Queueable Apex?
faq.answer: Queueable Apex supports non-primitive member variables and job chaining with better monitoring via AsyncApexJob. Batch Apex handles larger data volumes but has more complex syntax. Queueable is limited to 50 jobs per transaction.

faq.question: How do I prevent trigger recursion in Salesforce?
faq.answer: Use a static boolean flag in the trigger handler class to track execution state. Check and set this flag to ensure trigger logic runs only once per transaction, preventing infinite loops from workflow rules or process builder.

faq.question: Can this skill deploy code to my Salesforce org?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides development patterns and code guidance only. Deployment requires Salesforce DX, Change Sets, or CI/CD tools like Copado or Gearset to push code to target orgs.

faq.question: What Salesforce API versions does this skill support?
faq.answer: Patterns are compatible with current Salesforce API versions. LWC requires API version 45.0 or later. Always verify specific feature availability in Salesforce release notes for your org's version.
